From: Flavia Kupferberg

Dear Jeffrey,

If you must go back to beginning I am more than
happy to remind you of
our original agreement. I had originally asked for
my usual fee of $
20K/month, which I had agreed to reduce to $
15K/month. Your
counteroffer was $12K; to which I never agreed - my
original request of
$ 15K/month was as low as my fee could be reduced.
After I said no,
you decreased my fee further to $10K/month with the
stipulation that by
the end of our agreement, if you reached your goal,
you would give me a
bonus which would be more lucrative for me than the
15k/month - the
bonus should have amounted to more than $ 30K. I
agreed because I had
the utmost confidence in you and in my capabilities
as an experienced
fitness professional for achieving your goals.
Your goals were #1
improve posture #2 strengthen knee #3 lose 10lbs.
Following the fitness
evaluation, you were in total agreement that your
knee did indeed knee
strengthen, your posture improved and you lost over
15lbs of body fat
and gained 15lbs of muscle - building muscle is a
necessary requirement
to improve your posture and gain strength in your
knee; additionally,
you lost two (2) inches from your waist, improved
balance and increased
flexibility. Clearly my mission was accomplished
above and beyond your
original results. Your argument was you did not
lose 10lbs as indicated
by the weight scale, which for you meant you did not
achieve your goal.
If you were to ask any fitness professional they
would inform you
otherwise. Any money I received after our agreement
was for my a la
carte services to see both you and Ghilene. The
payment of $20K you
mentioned in your email was for a la carte sessions
for November,
December and January. After many weeks of going
back and forth,
negotiating my bonus, you transferred an additional
$10k (end o January
26th, 2006) to my account, which a few days later I
was told was for the
purchase of twenty (20) sessions @ $500/each for
personal training
sessions with me. I was informed that in the event
you did not use all

From: Flavia Kupferberg
[mailto:flavia@completebody.com]
Sent: Wednesday, September 20, 2006 9:49 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject:

Dear Jeffrey,

If you must go back to beginning I am more than
happy to remind you of
our original agreement. I had originally asked for
my usual fee of $
20K/month, which I had agreed to reduce to $
15K/month. Your
counteroffer was $12K; to which I never agreed - my
original request of
$ 15K/month was as low as my fee could be reduced.
After I said no,
you decreased my fee further to $10K/month with the
stipulation that by
the end of our agreement, if you reached your goal,
you would give me a
bonus which would be more lucrative for me than the
15k/month - the
bonus should have amounted to more than $ 30K. I
agreed because I had
the utmost confidence in you and in my capabilities
as an experienced
fitness professional for achieving your goals.
Your goals were #1
improve posture #2 strengthen knee #3 lose 10lbs.
Following the fitness
evaluation, you were in total agreement that your
knee did indeed knee
strengthen, your posture improved and you lost over
15lbs of body fat
and gained 15lbs of muscle - building muscle is a
necessary requirement
to improve your posture and gain strength in your
knee; additionally,
you lost two (2) inches from your waist, improved
balance and increased
flexibility. Clearly my mission was accomplished
above and beyond your
original results. Your argument was you did not
lose 10lbs as indicated
by the weight scale, which for you meant you did not
achieve your goal.
If you were to ask any fitness professional they
would inform you
otherwise. Any money I received after our agreement
was for my a la
carte services to see both you and Ghilene. The
payment of $20K you
mentioned in your email was for a la carte sessions
for November,
December and January. After many weeks of going
back and forth,
negotiating my bonus, you transferred an additional
$10k (end o January
26th, 2006) to my account, which a few days later I
was told was for the
purchase of twenty (20) sessions @ $500/each for
personal training
sessions with me. I was informed that in the event
you did not use all
twenty (20) sessions, the money would be mine to
keep. At this time,
more than half of this money was used for training
sessions. November,
December, January 20k a la carte sessions

Regarding the matter with Igor. Because of the
different payment
methods involving Igor, instead of getting his
yearly earnings ~
35K-40K, I consented to the basic 20% headhunter's
fee of his salary
which is $ 24K which barely compensated me the
significant amount of
money I suffered by not having him as a trainer for
my company.

Regarding your payments for my trips: it is without
question - I was
appreciative, but just to remind you, I lost a fair
amount of money when
I took the trip to Paris (I was not able to see any
clients) because I
did not think it safe for Tatiana to travel alone in
her condition. I
trained Ghilene while I was in Paris. With
attention to the trip to
Chicago you offered to pay for said trip in order to
attend a fitness
expo which would enhance our personal training
sessions.

I have equal respect for you and, as you are aware,
I had a great
experience while I was training you, but
unfortunately, financially I
cannot say the same. I honestly lost over 50k as a
result of losing
Igor as a trainer and rearranging my schedule to
accommodate your

--- David Mitchell djm@mitchellholdings.com wrote:

These pictures are from the New York Soho House
website that we clipped
to show you the bathtub in room idea

From: Tameka Ramsey
Sent: Friday, July 07, 2006 3:04 PM
To: David Mitchell
Subject:

For bathing fun there are freestanding stone colored
egg-shaped baths
reflected in giant 70-inch mirrors positioned
behind.

Egg-shaped baths have made their way into the
bedrooms and are placed in
front of huge 8 ft high Venetian mirrors, adding to
the fun of bathing.

The oversized bedrooms feature the exposed beams and
brick walls of the
former warehouse and have been designed with an
emphasis on sumptuous
materials, rich colors and sensual textures. Crystal
chandeliers,
antique furniture and oriental rugs are combined
with state-of-the-art
plasma screens, DVD players and high-speed internet
access.

In the larger rooms, freestanding baths are big
enough for two and all
bathrooms have spacious walk-in showers. Cowshed
hair and body products
are complimentary for guests during their stay. They
are made by hand in
England using organic flower and plant oils with
herbal essences.

Tameka Nikki Ramsey

Assistant to David Mitchell

TCC Billboard Artist Showcase - In conjunction with
Billboard Magazine, TCC
is presenting an exclusive, ultra-insider showcase
series. Once a month, we
will invite you to a performance by a cutting-edge
talent on the verge of
being discovered. Each showcase will feature artists
who are creating buzz
and attention, but who have not yet been signed by a
label. In this
intimate setting you will be listening to the next,
probable #1
singer-songwriter musician long before the world
knows they exist. See
details below for our first event on February 28th.

Tuesday, February 28th

TCC Billboard Artist Showcase Series

6:00 Reception

7:00 Performance

The first artist to perform in this exciting
collaboration between TCC and
Billboard Magazine is Wes Hutchinson.

Bridging pop and rock, Hutchinson's music evokes the
sensitivity of John
Lennon as well as the more robust sensibilities of
classic rock bands like
The Who and U2.

TCC is pleased to announce we will be screening all
five "Best Picture"
Oscar nominated movies starting Monday February
27th. Please see the
schedule of screenings below.

Capote

Monday February 27th

Two showings at 6PM & 8:30PM

Running time: 98 Minutes

Brokeback Mountain

Tuesday February 28th

Two showings at 6PM & 8:30PM

Running time: 134 Minutes

Crash

Wednesday March 1st

Showing at 6PM

Running time: 113 Minutes

Good Night and Good Luck

Wednesday March 1st

Showing at 8:30PM

Running time: 93 Minutes

Munich

Friday March 3rd

Showing at 7:30 PM

Running time: 164 Minutes
